description: A patient with chronic myeloid leukemia (CML) may receive either Dasatinib or Imatinib. The patient also takes Ketoconazole (antifungal prophylaxis), Warfarin (anticoagulation), and Amiodarone (atrial fibrillation rate control). Using the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app, check the interaction color for all six drug pairs (each cancer drug against each co-medication). For any Red-rated interactions, view the Interaction Details page and note the pharmacological mechanism. Determine which cancer drug has the safer overall interaction profile for this patient. For the recommended cancer drug, check whether safer co-medication alternatives exist: Fluconazole for Ketoconazole, Apixaban for Warfarin, or Bisoprolol for Amiodarone. Save a structured consultation report to /sdcard/Download/cml_drug_safety_report.txt documenting all interaction colors, mechanism notes for Red interactions, your cancer drug recommendation with reasoning, and any co-medication substitutions with their colors.

metadata.clinical_context: CML first-line TKI selection with polypharmacy safety review
